title: CEC's response was 'get lost': TMC's Derek O'Brien details meet with Gyanesh Kumar
content: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) accused Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar of partisanship and corruption after a brief meeting. The party alleged their delegation was dismissed abruptly and that the EC had ignored nine letters from Mamata Banerjee. The TMC also presented evidence of alleged BJP links among Bengal officials, demanding their transfer.

title: Polls in Kerala, Assam, Puducherry tomorrow: Here's how to check your name in voter list
content: Voters in Kerala, Assam, and Puducherry can now verify their names on the electoral roll. The Election Commission offers online tools for easy checking. Voters can use their EPIC number or personal details. Registered mobile numbers also facilitate verification. Downloading the complete voter list for polling booths is also possible. This advance check helps streamline the voting process.

title: Beer party during Ganga boat ride in Varanasi raises eyebrows
content: A boat decorator was arrested in Varanasi after a video of him drinking alcohol on the Ganga river surfaced online. This incident follows a previous viral video showing an iftar party's waste allegedly dumped into the river. Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav criticized the ruling BJP, linking increased alcohol consumption to citizen stress.

title: FBI and NSA issue joint 'warning' of Iran-linked cyberattacks on critical infrastructure
content: US agencies have issued a stark warning about escalating cyberattacks from Iran-linked hackers targeting critical infrastructure like water and energy. These sophisticated attacks aim to disrupt operations and cause financial losses by exploiting vulnerabilities in industrial control systems. Companies are urged to immediately review security measures to prevent further compromise.

title: Jasprit Bumrah’s unwanted hat-trick: First time in 8 years, just fourth ever
content: Amid the Sooryavanshi-Jaiswal mayhem, Bumrah returned wicketless for the third consecutive match. A rarity in Bumrah’s illustrious T20 career. For Bumrah, this unwanted hat-trick of going wicketless for three matches in a row was in fact the first time in nearly 8 years and only the fourth time in his entire T20 career.

title: Several children injured as school bus overturns in Meerut
content: A school bus carrying approximately 35 students overturned near the Kali river in Meerut on Wednesday morning, resulting in injuries to at least 10 children. Local residents quickly responded to the scene, assisting in the rescue of the students from the overturned bus.

title: Bengal poll battle heats up: EC and TMC exchange 'straight talk'
content: The Election Commission of India has assured that the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections will be conducted without fear, violence, or intimidation. This statement follows a meeting with the TMC, which accused the ECI of bias towards the BJP and alleged systematic disenfranchisement of voters through electoral roll revisions.

title: Canada has changed immigration rules; changing visa fee, work permit rules and more
content: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting in April 2026 with increased passport and citizenship fees, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain more control over candidate assessment, and rural employers can hire more foreign workers. Saskatchewan introduces new application fees for its nominee program.

title: Stealth, speed & sensor fusion: Why F-22 Raptor is the most lethal fighter jet ever built
content: The F-22 Raptor, a fifth-generation fighter, continues to dominate aerial combat with its stealth, speed, and advanced avionics. This US Air Force marvel excels in detecting and engaging threats before being seen, ensuring air superiority. Its unique combination of capabilities, including supercruise and exceptional maneuverability, makes it a formidable asset even against newer platforms.

title: First in 7 years! India to get Iran crude oil on US sanctions waiver
content: India, the world’s third-largest importer and consumer of oil, has not taken any shipments from Iran since May 2019, when US sanctions led it to halt such purchases.

title: Dhurandhar 2 BO day 21: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1050 cr milestone
content: Aditya Dhar’s espionage epic, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge,' continues to script history at the global box office. On day 21, Wednesday, April 8, 2026, the Ranveer Singh-led thriller solidified its legendary status by becoming the first Hindi film ever to surpass the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Having already outpaced giants like 'Jawan' and 'KGF: Chapter 2', the film has now amassed a domestic net of Rs. 1,034.00 crore. Despite the ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film remains a powerhouse, eyeing a Rs. 1,700 crore worldwide gross target.

title: Sam Altman's 13-page AI vision 'supports' Vinod Khosla's 'no income tax' for Americans idea
content: OpenAI's new policy paper warns current economic systems are unprepared for advanced AI, proposing tax reforms and a Public Wealth Fund for citizen benefit. This aligns with Vinod Khosla's call to eliminate income taxes for those earning under $100,000 and tax all income equally, suggesting a shift towards capital-based revenue to fund social programs and ensure shared AI-driven growth.

title: 'Israel backs US ceasefire with Iran but not Hezbollah': What Netanyahu said on two-week truce
content: Israel backs President Trump's two-week pause on Iran strikes, provided Tehran reopens vital shipping lanes and halts regional attacks.  However, Prime Minister Netanyahu stressed this ceasefire explicitly excludes Lebanon, indicating continued operations on that front.  This stance contrasts with Iran's broader peace proposal, highlighting ongoing regional complexities and differing interpretations of the truce.

title: Kim's rare praise, Lee's apology and then a barrage of missiles: Why did North Korea fire two rounds
content: North Korea fired multiple ballistic missiles on Wednesday. This followed a rare conciliatory message from leader Kim Jong Un to South Korean President Lee Jae Myung. Seoul convened an emergency meeting and urged Pyongyang to stop violating UN resolutions. Analysts suggest North Korea is asserting strength while seeking nuclear power recognition.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to MI next? Ex-India cricketer’s post sparks buzz
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is creating a stir, with a social media post hinting at a potential future with the Mumbai Indians. The 15-year-old prodigy has rewritten T20 records, becoming the youngest centurion and showcasing a fearless approach against top bowlers like Jasprit Bumrah.

title: 'Sincere regret': Kharge apologises for 'people illiterate in Gujarat' remark
content: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge has apologized for his recent remarks targeting people of Gujarat, stating his comments were misinterpreted and he never intended to cause offense. The apology follows sharp criticism from the BJP, which deemed his statements insulting and unacceptable, highlighting Gujarat's literacy and contributions.

title: IPL 2026: CSK sweat on MS Dhoni’s fitness with no clear return date
content: Chennai Super Kings face growing uncertainty over MS Dhoni's availability for IPL 2026. The veteran wicketkeeper-batter is undergoing rehabilitation for a calf muscle strain and is unlikely to be match-ready soon. His absence coincides with CSK's poor start, having lost their first three matches, highlighting a critical gap in crunch situations.

title: Thief hides in pond for 5 hours to evade arrest, uses lotus stem to breathe
content: An inter-state thief, Harvinder Singh, was apprehended by Railway Protection Force (RPF) in Jabalpur after a five-hour underwater evasion using a lotus stem. Singh, a notorious offender targeting train passengers, was eventually pulled from a pond and overpowered despite attempts to flee. He is wanted in multiple states for theft and snatching cases.

title: '3 glasses of wine and...': Details of Warner's drunk driving arrest revealed
content: Former Australian opener David Warner has been charged with drink driving in Maroubra, Sydney, after a random breath test revealed a blood alcohol reading of 0.104. He was pulled over by police and subsequently arrested, with a court appearance scheduled for May 7, 2026. This incident occurred during a break from his PSL 2026 commitments.

title: ‘Khel bindaas’: Jaiswal’s mantra fuels Sooryavanshi’s fearless IPL blitz
content: Opening batter Yashasvi Jaiswal's 'khel bindaas' mantra fueled Rajasthan Royals' fearless 27-run win over Mumbai Indians. Jaiswal, Player of the Match for his unbeaten 77, revealed how positive messaging with opening partner Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who smashed Jasprit Bumrah for six first ball, set the tone for their dominant 80-run partnership.

title: 'Derogatory, communal': Delhi HC asks Centre, police to take action against Rana Ayyub
content: The Delhi High Court has directed social media platform X, the central government, and Delhi Police to act on journalist Rana Ayyub's alleged derogatory tweets about Hindu deities and Vinayak Damodar Savarkar. The court deemed the posts "derogatory, inflammatory and communal," seeking responses by April 10.

title: Iran-US ceasefire impact: Oil prices drop, Hormuz to see 'controlled' movement; global markets surge
content: A temporary ceasefire between Iran and the US is reshaping global markets. Oil prices have dropped significantly as the Strait of Hormuz sees cautious reopening. Stock markets worldwide are experiencing a strong rally, with Indian markets showing substantial gains. This development offers relief from geopolitical tensions and supports economic stability.

title: 'Self-styled vishwaguru stands exposed': Congress targets PM Modi over Iran-US ceasefire
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh criticized PM Modi's foreign policy, questioning the timing of his Israel visit before military action against Iran and the perceived failure of Operation Sindoor's global outreach. Ramesh argued that Pakistan's role in a ceasefire undermined Modi's personalized diplomacy and his strategy to isolate Islamabad, contrasting it with Manmohan Singh's success after the 26/11 attacks.

title: KVS Balvatika lottery result 2026 released: Check steps to download here
content: The Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan has announced the Balvatika Lottery Result 2026 for the 2026–27 session, covering admissions to Balvatika-1 and 3. Provisional selection and waitlists are now accessible online. The lottery system aims to ensure fair allocation of limited seats, with parents required to check results and complete further admission formalities within deadlines.

title: Atmanirbhar atom: Why India’s fast breeder nuclear reactor PFBR is a game-changer
content: India achieved a significant milestone as its indigenously developed 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam attained criticality. This breakthrough signals a crucial step towards full-scale power generation and enhances India's fuel self-reliance, positioning it among nations with advanced breeder reactor technology.

title: While BSF father guards nation, kids killed in sleep; Manipur onslaught on
content: A powerful blast tore through a home in Bishnupur district, killing two young children, five-year-old Oinam Tomthin and his infant sister Oinam Yaisana, as they slept. Their mother is critically injured, and the incident has sparked widespread public outrage and protests against the ongoing violence in the region.

title: Sooryavanshi: How Akshay Karanje, RR’s talent spotter, unearthed Bihar’s boy wonder
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is electrifying the IPL with his audacious batting, fearlessly taking on top bowlers like Jasprit Bumrah. Rajasthan Royals' scouting efforts, spearheaded by Akshay Karanje and endorsed by Kumar Sangakkara, identified this prodigious talent, shifting the franchise's focus towards nurturing young stars for consistent performance and national benefit.

title: BMW India posts best-ever Q1 17% growth: 2nd fastest growing market globally
content: BMW Group India plans to launch 27 new products in 2026 across BMW, MINI and BMW Motorrad, including new-generation models, facelifts and limited editions. Recent launches include the BMW M2 CS, BMW X3 30, BMW M 1000 R and MINI Cooper S Victory Edition, with eight more planned in the second quarter.

title: US-Iran war impact: RBI governor flags 5 risks for India
content: The central bank governor is of the view that this will provide the Indian economy with ‘greater resilience’ to withstand shocks. However, the downside risks to growth projections remain, especially in case of a prolonged West Asia conflict.

title: Tensions continue in Middle East despite ceasefire; Israel says deal limited to Iran
content: Missile alerts sounded across the Middle East despite a reported two-week ceasefire between Iran and the United States. Fighting continued on multiple fronts, with a gas facility in Abu Dhabi ablaze following Iranian strikes. The ceasefire's terms and implementation remain uncertain, with Iran and Israel appearing to continue military actions.

title: Japanese tourist molested in Jaipur: 1 arrested, minor detained; hunt on for 3 others
content: Jaipur police swiftly apprehended one suspect and detained a minor following the alleged harassment of a Japanese tourist near Jaigarh Fort.  A manhunt is underway for three other identified individuals involved in the Sunday morning incident.  Authorities are prioritizing swift prosecution, with over 50 CCTV footages scanned and technical surveillance employed to track the remaining accused.

title: 'Bumrah had no plan': Pathan explains how Sooryavanshi forced world's best to rethink
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stunned cricket fans by hitting two sixes off world-class bowler Jasprit Bumrah in a single IPL over.  Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan lauded the young prodigy's fearless approach, noting how Sooryavanshi's audacious hitting forced even Bumrah to rethink his strategy.  This remarkable feat against a top bowler highlights the youngster's immense potential.

title: Piyush Group real estate crisis: 1,500 buyers, unfinished homes, and a maze of land deals
content: Thousands of homebuyers in Haryana face years of delay as Piyush Group projects remain unfinished. Allegations suggest buyer funds were diverted for new land acquisitions, with promoters reportedly transferring project assets to family during insolvency. The Enforcement Directorate has filed a chargesheet and attached properties worth Rs 944 crore.

title: Can Tehran charge toll fee in Strait of Hormuz? What international law says
content: Iran's proposal to levy transit fees on ships in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global oil route, is emerging as a new flashpoint. This move, aimed at generating revenue and exerting geopolitical leverage, challenges international maritime law and could disrupt global trade, pushing energy markets into uncertainty. Nations like India have rejected the idea.

title: Sooryavanshi stuns Bumrah, does what Samson, Abhishek, Gill couldn’t in T20s
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a remarkable feat against Jasprit Bumrah in the IPL, smashing two sixes off the formidable bowler in his very first encounter. This electrifying performance saw the young batsman score 39 off 14 balls, a feat no other prominent T20 star has managed against Bumrah.

title: 2 kids die after eating dosa from store bought batter: What went wrong, safety tips
content: Two young girls tragically died in Ahmedabad after consuming dosa made from store-bought fermented batter. The family fell ill with severe vomiting, leading to the deaths of the children while the parents remain hospitalized. Authorities have collected batter samples for analysis following the suspected food poisoning incident.

title: RBI policy: Why did MPC keep repo rate unchanged? RBI governor explains
content: According to RBI governor Malhotra, high frequency indicators till February, 2026 suggest the continuation of strong momentum in economic activity. Growth impulses continue to be supported by robust private consumption and investment demand.

title: Pune woman kills 11-month-old baby to start 'new life'; held after husband asks for child
content: A 22-year-old woman was arrested after allegedly killing her 11-month-old son to start a new life. She reportedly dumped his body in a well and remarried without informing her second husband about the child. Her former husband's missing person complaint led to her apprehension and confession.

title: Trump predicts 'positive action', 'big money' after truce; US to 'hang around' Strait of Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump declared a "total and complete victory" after announcing a ceasefire with Iran, ending 38 days of Operation Epic Fury. Both nations claimed success, with Iran pointing to Washington's acceptance of its 10-point proposal. The deal, brokered with Pakistan's mediation and potentially China's influence, addresses nuclear concerns and the Strait of Hormuz, though Israel's support has caveats regarding Hezbollah.

title: Goa nightclub fire tragedy: Luthra brothers to walk out of jail
content: Goa court grants bail to Gaurav and Saurabh Luthra in the NOC forgery case related to Birch by Romeo Lane nightclub. The Luthra brothers, owners of the venue where a fire killed 25, were already out on bail for the fire tragedy. Their release is now cleared in this separate case as well.

title: Mavs dispute claim they erred with Reaves' MRI
content: The Mavericks disputed Lakers coach JJ Redick's claim that their medical team examined the wrong part of Austin Reaves' body when the Lakers guard received an MRI by the Mavs' staff.

title: Soler, Lopez trade haymakers, ejected in brawl
content: Angels DH Jorge Soler charged the mound after Braves starter Reynaldo Lopez threw a high inside pitch, as both players threw punches amid a bench-clearing brawl in the fifth inning of Tuesday's game.

title: NBA vet Malone sees 'special' opportunity at UNC
content: New Tar Heels coach Michael Malone called North Carolina the only college job he would've accepted, saying he recognizes he has a "chance to be a part of something special" there.

title: Colorado QB's BAC twice legal limit in fatal crash
content: Colorado quarterback Dominiq Ponder had a blood alcohol level twice the legal limit for driving under the influence when he died in a single-car crash near Boulder on March 1.

title: Geno apologizes to Staley, says 'I lost myself'
content: UConn Huskies coach Geno Auriemma said in a statement Tuesday night that he lost himself with his behavior toward South Carolina coach Dawn Staley during his team's Final Four defeat last Friday.

title: Avalanche clinch Central, secure West's top seed
content: The Colorado Avalanche defeated the St. Louis Blues 3-1 on Tuesday night to clinch Central Division and secure the No. 1 seed in the Western Conference, but after the win, coach Jared Bednar was quick to caution his team is "not all the way there yet."

title: Tatum preps for MSG return: 'Just another game'
content: Jayson Tatum said that although he's not thrilled to return to the arena where he tore an Achilles less than a year ago, he is aiming to treat Thursday's matchup against the Knicks at MSG as "just another game."

title: Reinsdorf: Next Bulls GM must be sold on Donovan
content: Bulls CEO and president Michael Reinsdorf, who is looking for a new head of basketball operations after firing Arturas Karnisovas earlier this week, said Tuesday that retaining Billy Donovan as head coach would be a major requirement.

title: 🏈 Schrager's first-round NFL mock draft
content: Peter Schrager leans on his intel after last week's annual league meeting to project picks in the NFL draft. Here's where things stand right now in Round 1.

title: Top storylines: Scottie Scheffler, Bryson DeChambeau and sleeper contenders
content: Is Scottie Scheffler still the favorite? Can Bryson DeChambeau break through? How hard could the course play? We break down what to watch at the 2026 Masters.

title: Baseball in Latin America is broken -- is an international draft the answer?
content: As the search for the next Dominican baseball phenom intensifies, the tragic death of a teenager is the latest symptom of a failing system.

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: Ranking the best teams for next season, now
content: The 2026 men's hoops champion has been crowned. We're rolling right into ranking next season's best teams -- with a few new rules.

title: What's next for UConn, Michigan after championship: Recruits, transfer portal, more
content: Here's how next season is taking shape for every team that made the final rounds of the NCAA tournament.

title: Real Madrid say they can beat Bayern Munich after poor Champions League first leg. Are they right?
content: Lacking Bernabéu magic, Real Madrid can tee up a more spectacular comeback in Munich in the Champions League. They've done it before.

title: Inside the Bucks' lost season, and the looming 'nasty breakup' with Giannis Antetokounmpo
content: A difficult season exacerbated a 13-year relationship that was already on the rocks, leading to more questions than answers in Milwaukee.

title: PGA Tour vs. LIV in a much different place than it was at 2023 Masters
content: It's been three years, and while the LIV-PGA fight isn't necessarily 'won,' it certainly seems settled.

title: Michigan's championship punctuated Dusty May's homecoming in a full family affair
content: Just two seasons after arriving in Ann Arbor, May returned to his home state of Indiana to cut down the nets.

title: 'I started to love basketball again': How Michigan built a champion on fun and freedom
content: In the year of the freshman, Dusty May led a band of transfers and upperclassmen to a national title.

title: NBA playoff watch: Timberwolves look to clinch
content: Minnesota can lock up a top-six spot Tuesday. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Monday.

title: WNBA 2026 free agency: Predictions, biggest questions, more
content: Who are the biggest names who might be on the move, and how will the condensed offseason impact free agency?

title: Connelly's favorite transfer classes: Teams that pushed the envelope in the portal
content: Looking beyond the numbers, here are the teams that took the boldest steps in their portal approaches.

title: Ranking the top 20 men's basketball players to enter the transfer portal
content: More than 1,000 players have entered the portal less than 24 hours after it opened. Here's the top talent.

title: Tiger Woods isn't at Augusta this week, yet Tiger Woods is everywhere this week
content: After the recent tumult, Woods won't be in Augusta, but Masters fans still seek the ghosts of past miracle shots. There is no unwinding this tournament and Tiger.

title: Bruno Fernandes is 31 and Man United's MVP, but they shouldn't extend his contract
content: Bruno Fernandes' brilliant season means that Man United's decision to extend his contract should be an obvious one, right? Except, looking at how things played out with Liverpool and Mo Salah, it's not so simple.

title: Michael Malone to UNC?! How it happened, next steps for Tar Heels
content: What should Malone's top priorities be? Here are the biggest questions about the next chapter in Chapel Hill.

title: Five takeaways from NCAA regionals -- and what they mean for nationals
content: Chiles and Chio continued their superstar ways. Oklahoma, Florida and LSU dominated. After NCAA gymnastics regionals, the final eight teams are set.

title: Prospect watch: Konnor Griffin's here, who's next?
content: All the prospects fantasy managers should be keeping an eye on as the season moves through its first month.

title: Boxing divisional rankings: Why Wilder's win wasn't enough to join top 10 at heavyweight
content: The Deontay Wilder-Derek Chisora fight card caused a major shakeup, but not in the division you would think. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: NWSL Power Rankings: San Diego leads the way, Washington back on track
content: San Diego Wave are the new leaders of the ESPN's NWSL Power Rankings, while the Washington Spirit are soaring up.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Liverpool, Barcelona eye Italy defender
content: Liverpool and Barcelona are lining up a move to sign Atalanta and Italy international right back Marco Palestra. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Pickups: Jeremiah Fears, Will Riley among top adds for final week
content: Here are the players who can help fill holes on your roster during the final week of the fantasy basketball season.

title: Discussed ways to build strong future of India-U.S. ties: U.S. envoy Gor on meeting with Trump
content: ‘We discussed President Donald Trump's unwavering determination to bring global stability’ said the U.S. Ambassador

title: Kerala Assembly elections: On silent campaign day, war of words rages on
content: Opposing campaigns in Kerala indulge in muckraking in last-minute bid to recast electoral agenda to their advantage as the clock winds down to voting day on Thursday. In Palakkad, LDF and UDF accuse NDA of buying votes

title: Consumer court tells insurer to pay claim of Udupi boat stricken by Cyclone Phyan in Karwar that drifted to Maharashtra
content: After a legal battle of more than 16 years, Karnataka State Consumer Disputed Redressal Commission directed the insurance company to pay 75% of the claim, fine and litigation costs

title: Govt. extends bulk commercial LPG allocation to more sectors
content: Petroleum Ministry caps bulk non-domestic LPG allocation at 0.2 TMT per day; more industries allowed up to 70% of pre-March 2026 consumption

title: Rediscovering Vaanam: How a contemporary festival in Tamil Nadu celebrates Dalit History Month
content: Four editions in, Neelam’s annual Dalit History Month celebration Vaanam, gets a new lease of life, with a competition category in their film festival, some dramatics, a literary festival, and an art exhibit

title: Kharge expresses regret over 'illiterate' remark, says he has highest of respect for Gujaratis
content: Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge apologises for calling Gujaratis ‘illiterate’, reaffirming his respect for the State’s people

title: SC hearing on Sabarimala women entry LIVE: Concept of logic can't be applied to religion, says Justice Sundresh
content: Supreme Court is witnessing an interesting debate on what is an 'essential' practice and what can be termed as 'superstition', and when can courts intervene

title: Farmers seek lift irrigation project from Moyar as water scarcity grips Thalavadi
content: Several panchayats, including Bynapuram, Diginarai, Arulavadi, and Thalavadi, are facing acute drought, forcing residents to travel to neighbouring villages for water

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026 | Interviews with key candidates and political leaders
content: A collection of interviews by ‘The Hindu’ with key candidates and political leaders as part of Kerala Assembly elections 2026 coverage

title: When historic markers in Chennai are demolished without explanation
content: The descendants of Dr. M.R. Gurusamy Mudaliar are asking as to why his name has been removed from a block at the Kilpauk Medical College (KMC) when the structure was rebuilt recently

title: Mamata Banerjee files nomination from Bhabanipur; sets stage for high-stakes contest
content: CM Mamata Banerjee is set for a high-stakes poll contest against rival BJP candidate Suvendu Adhikari in Bhabanipur Assembly seat

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Pinarayi Vijayan and Revanth Reddy skirmish on X over use of coarse language in politics
content: Telangana CM’s use of a “schoolyard taunt,” a contemporary adaptation of a popular dialogue from Mohanlal-starrer Narasimham, Nee Po Mone Vijayan, instead of the original, Nee Po Mone Dinesha, provokes social media altercation

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Congress leaders criticise Pinarayi Vijayan’s remarks against Revanth Reddy
content: Senior Congress leader Thiruvanchoor Radhakrishnan, contesting from Kottayam, joked that the Chief Minister was ‘abusing someone’ on his way out. K. Muraleedharan, the party candidate from Vattiyurkavu, echoed that the Chief Minister was setting ‘a poor example’ for the younger generation

title: Telangana Transco prepares for peak summer power demand
content: Chairing a review meeting of Transco and Operations wings at the OMC Circle Office here on Tuesday, Mr. Rao made it clear that officials would be held accountable for maintaining zero interruptions at all substations across the region.

title: Indian embassy in Iran asks citizens to ‘expeditiously exit’, hours after ceasefire announcement
content: The Indian embassy also said that citizens’ exit must be in coordination with the embassy, and shared emergency numbers

title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah questions gains of U.S. in West Asia conflict after ceasefire deal
content: Omar Abdullah remarked that the passage was already “open and freely available” before the war began.

title: Goa nightclub fire: Luthra brothers granted bail, set to walk out of jail
content: The Luthra brothers, facing serious allegations of forgery linked to their nightclub’s operations and a tragic fire that claimed 25 lives, are now free on bail as they await further legal proceedings

title: Uppal police refute ‘ambulance delay’ claims, say no prolonged traffic disruption after IPL match
content: The police clarified that while the IPL match between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Lucknow Super Giants at the Rajiv Gandhi International Cricket Stadium concluded around 7 p.m., leading to the movement of nearly 25,000 spectators, the resulting congestion was temporary.

title: Delimitation will cause economic hardship to South Indian States, says Congress leader D.K. Suresh
content: Former MP alleges that the delimitation of Lok Sabha and Assembly constituencies would not only reduce the number of seats for South Indian States, but would also lead to serious economic hardship

title: Maharashtra government pushes infra projects, promises traffic decongestion in Mumbai
content: MMRDA’s 11.84 km twin-tunnel project is a six-lane road connecting Borivali in Mumbai and Ghodbunder Road in Thane, to be constructed beneath Sanjay Gandhi National Park

title: Pinarayi Vijayan’s political career timeline
content: As Kerala gears up for the crucial 2026 Assembly elections, Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan finds himself at a pivotal crossroads in his political career

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: A do-or-die battle for major fronts, their skippers
content: Having been in the political wilderness for a decade, the UDF  cannot afford to sit out of power for another five years. For the LDF, which suffered a series of setbacks in the Assembly bypolls, the Lok Sabha elections, and the local body polls, the April 9 Assembly elections will be a test of its credentials.  For the National Democratic Alliance too the polls will be decisive, having seen a slump in their vote share in the local body elections

title: Assembly Elections 2026: Know the documents required for voting
content: If you are voting in these elections, it is important to know the documents that you can use to cast your vote on the day of polling.

title: Accused in Kerala’s Walayar mob lynching case found dead
content: Vinod Kumar was out on bail, and Kerala High Court was scheduled to consider an application by the SIT seeking cancellation of his bail

title: Uttar Pradesh govt hikes honorarium of Shiksha Mitras, part-time instructors
content: Shiksha Mitra’s honorarium increased from ₹10,000 to ₹18,000; Part-time instructors’ honorarium increased from ₹9,000 to ₹17,000

title: Special session on delimitation: T.N. CM Stalin says ‘DMK will not stand by and watch’
content: Stalin asked why there is a hurry to convene a special session of Parliament right in the middle of elections to five States

title: Friends of Police: a community policing initiative that was shut down in Tamil Nadu after Sattankulam custodial deaths
content: What started as a small team of volunteers calling itself ‘Shore Care Committees’ and later ‘Friends of Police’ to guard the shores of Ramanathapuram district later proliferated to spread across the State of Tamil Nadu till 2020 when it was disbanded.

title: Abandon fast breeder nuclear reactor project at Kalpakkam, Vaiko urges Centre
content: He said the technology used in this type of reactor to generate power had been tested in several countries, but had been largely abandoned due to safety concerns and high economic costs

title: Bengaluru: One dead in fatal early morning truck collision near Satellite Bus Stand
content: Police said the truck was descending the Mysuru Road flyover when the driver reportedly lost control and crashed into a 10-wheel lorry that was stopped at the signal.

title: Southern districts of Tamil Nadu, once known as a laggard, seek to shed the bad image
content: In the last eight months, two important developments took place in Thoothukudi itself: the establishment of an electric vehicle plant and the commissioning of the first 660-MW supercritical thermal power plant. The Thoothukudi port has initiated tendering for the outer harbour

title: A plastics ban Tamil Nadu tried in 2002 and stepped back from
content: Introduced on May 7, 2002, the Bill proposed a ban on the sale, storage, transport and use of non-reusable plastics due to the rapid increase in disposable plastic consumption and its impact on groundwater contamination and rainwater percolation

title: NCB tells Bombay HC its inquiry into Wankhede based on non-anonymous complaints
content: Wankhede has filed a petition before the High Court challenging the probe into his conduct in drug-related cases, including the one connected to the death of actor Sushant Singh Rajput

title: Rapid test for antimicrobial resistance in infections launched in Guntur
content: Ipseity Diagnostics launches a rapid AMR PCR panel in Guntur, enabling swift detection of drug resistance in infections

title: Kerala elections: Win or lose, Congress could ‘transition into BJP’ in Kerala, alleges CPI(M) leader M. Swaraj
content: Swaraj cites instances of Congress MLAs joining BJP in different States and warns the electorate in Kerala that such a scenario is possible in the State too

title: How West Bengal voted in 2021: a recap

title: Extended timeline ensured accurate voter list: U.P. CEO

title: Poor delivery of schemes, lack of support for the farm sector, major talking points in Madurantakam

title: Behold, the 'Zammoth': Utah Mammoth debut new fan attraction at Delta Center

title: Women's transfer rankings for 2026-27 season
